title: Periodic automated cleanup of completed tasks and ideas
summary: Completed and cancelled entries don't need to live in the active store indefinitely — everything is in git history. A periodic cleanup (e.g. daily, weekly, or N days after marking done) deletes done/cancelled entries from the JSONL and .md files, keeping the active store lean. If historical data is ever needed, a domus task history command could retrieve it from git. Key open questions: what trigger (time-based vs manual vs on-exit), what threshold (immediate, N days, never for certain tags), and whether to build the git-retrieval path before or after enabling cleanup.

title: Domus Skills Plugin System
summary: Package Claude skills (capture-task, capture-idea, etc.) inside the domus repo and install them via a domus command, so skills are versioned alongside the CLI they wrap.

title: Subagent Strategy for Domus Workflow
summary: Define where Claude subagents add value in the domus/Oracle system without polluting the main conversation context. Which steps (refinement, scoping, task dispatch) benefit from delegation?

title: Domus as the sole gateway to index and task/idea data
summary: Ensure index files (tasks.jsonl, ideas.jsonl) are never loaded into Claude's context directly. Domus CLI should provide all access. Individual md files should only load when text edits are needed, and even then prefer subagents.

title: Testing strategy for domus
summary: How to test the domus CLI and workflow system. Unit tests are straightforward but not yet implemented. E2E testing is unclear — use an existing harness or write a custom one? Some functionality may also need deeper integration-level analysis.

title: Session orientation - how does a new session know what to do next
summary: A new Claude session can run domus task ready to find actionable tasks, but lacks context about priority, recent decisions, and the broader focus area. Need a session-start ritual and/or memory strategy so a fresh session can orient itself without a lengthy briefing.

title: Spec system - behavioural contracts for the codebase
summary: Specs describe what the system should do (intent), while code describes what it actually does (reality). A two-folder model (future/ and current/) lets domus promote spec changes atomically on task completion. A self-healing doctor system catches drift. Spec granularity is the open question.

title: Plans and autonomous tasks are the same artifact
summary: A plan detailed enough for Claude to execute and an autonomous-refinement task contain identical information in different templates. The capture-task skill should produce autonomous-quality tasks directly from a sufficiently detailed scoping conversation, skipping the plan artifact.

title: Global vs project-local domus workspace
summary: Allow domus to operate against a global workspace separate from the current project directory, so .domus/ data isn't polluted into unrelated repos.
outcome_note: Tried building this before and it didn't work. Deferring until there's a concrete use case that makes the right resolution strategy obvious.

title: Domus codebase architecture document
summary: A lightweight ARCHITECTURE.md committed to the domus repo that maps what each file/module does and why, so future sessions can orient quickly without reading all source files.

title: Oracle session UX: pick-then-launch vs launch-then-pick
summary: Unresolved UX question about how the idea refinement session is initiated. Option A: user picks idea first, then Oracle session launches with that idea as context. Option B: Oracle session launches first, lists ideas, user picks from within the session.

title: ADR support in the spec system
summary: Specs describe what the system does (behavioural contracts). ADRs describe why a particular implementation approach was chosen. These belong together — the 'why' informs future refactoring and enhancements by making the original constraints visible. Explore how ADRs sit alongside or within spec files.

title: Interactive session model and launch command design
summary: Current 'work' and 'idea' commands launch separate sessions per persona. Alternative model: one session loads all personas, switch organically via natural language (e.g. 'as the Oracle, let's discuss X'). Either way, commands should follow 'domus launch <persona>' pattern. Must also distinguish interactive (human-present) vs background (worker) launches explicitly — these work very differently and need separate command paths.

title: Standardize idea MD file format to match task MD conventions
summary: Idea MD files use an old ad-hoc format (Date, Project headers) while task MDs have Status, Refinement, Priority, Captured fields kept in sync by the CLI. Should idea MDs adopt the same structure for consistency and doctor-skill cross-checking?
